one_time_description: Treaty loan forgiveness - $2,044,583 in comprehensive land claim negotiation debt owed to Carrier Sekani Tribal Council forgiven by the Federal Government
economic_notes: Nation separated from Binche Whut'en effective April 1, 2019. Tanizul Timber Ltd. (100% owned) reported net income of $860,394 on revenue of $1,090,897. COVID-19 noted as significant event with unknown future impact.
notes: Qualified audit opinion due to: (1) separation from Binche Whut'en on April 1, 2019 with uncertain amounts for housing rental arrears and accommodation agreement revenues; (2) budget not formally approved as required by PSAS. Nation completed major restructuring transferring $4,148,031 in net assets to Binche Whut'en. Tanizul Timber Ltd. accounted for by modified equity method (GBE). CMHC Social Housing Program is the only consolidated subsidiary. Net financial assets figure of $6,863,439 is the actual year-end balance per financial statements. Tangible capital assets net book value not explicitly stated on face of balance sheet in the extracted text. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $7,702,221 + Provincial $4,659,950 + FNHA $2,248304 + Other federal $197,584 + CMHC $66,707 = $14,874,766; OSR = $19,426,345 - $14,874,766 = $4,551,579. Note: Treaty loan forgiveness may be considered a government transfer. Capital Projects expenses $30,411 and Social Housing expenses $162,173 included in total but not mapped to standard categories.
